name: Shadowbroker
description: Open-source intelligence for the global theater. Track everything from the corporate/private jets of the wealthy, and spy satellites, to seismic events in one unified interface. The knowledge is available to all but rarely aggregated in the open, until now.
readme_summary: ShadowBroker is a real-time, multi-domain OSINT dashboard that aggregates live data from dozens of open-source intelligence feeds and displays them on a unified map interface. It tracks aircraft, ships, satellites, earthquakes, conflict zones, CCTV networks, and geopolitical events, updating all information in real time.
key_features: Tracks aircraft, ships, satellites, earthquakes, conflict zones, CCTV networks, GPS jamming, and geopolitical events
Live data aggregation from dozens of open-source intelligence feeds
Built with Next.js, MapLibre GL, FastAPI, and Python
Comprehensive maritime tracking (AIS, carrier strike groups)
Orbital tracking of satellites with mission-type classification
Geopolitical incident aggregation and conflict zone monitoring
primary_use_case: To provide a unified, real-time geospatial intelligence platform for monitoring global activities across various domains, including aviation, maritime, space, and geopolitical events.
cover_image_prompt: A highly detailed, stylized world map displayed on a futuristic, holographic console. From various points on the map, streams of light representing different data types (planes, ships, satellites, seismic waves, news feeds) converge towards the center, where they are organized and displayed as clear, distinct icons and information panels. A subtle, glowing outline of the 'ShadowBroker' logo is integrated into the console's interface. The overall mood is one of comprehensive global awareness and control, with a dark, sophisticated color palette and glowing digital elements. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clarity and visual storytelling.

name: Cosmos-Server
description: ☁️ The Most Secure and Easy Selfhosted Home Server. Take control of your data and privacy without sacrificing security and stability  (Authentication, anti-DDOS, anti-bot)
readme_summary: Cosmos is a self-hosted home server solution designed to enhance the security and ease of managing personal applications. It acts as a secure gateway and server manager, offering features like an app store, storage management, reverse proxy, and authentication server to protect self-hosted services.
key_features: App Store for easy installation and management
Storage Manager with Parity Disks and MergerFS
Authentication Server with MFA and multiple strategies
primary_use_case: Securing and simplifying the management of self-hosted applications on a home server.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing a vast, bustling library filled with diverse books representing various applications (Plex, HomeAssistant, blogs). The librarian is using a magical, glowing 'Cosmos' book as a central hub, which automatically sorts, secures, and provides easy access to all other books. Some books are neatly shelved, others are being scanned for security, and a few are being remotely accessed via glowing portals. Subtle UI elements showing security shields and access logs are integrated into the library's architecture. The scene is set in a warm, inviting study with soft, ambient lighting, rendered in a detailed 3D isometric illustration style.

name: hexhog
readme_summary: Hexhog is a configurable hex viewer and editor written in Rust. It allows users to inspect and modify the byte-level content of files directly, offering basic editing features like byte insertion, deletion, copying, and pasting.
key_features: Installation via cargo, AUR, and Homebrew
primary_use_case: Editing and inspecting the raw byte content of files.
cover_image_prompt: A meticulous cartographer in a dimly lit, ancient library, using a magnifying glass to examine a rolled-up parchment. The parchment is covered in a dense grid of cryptic symbols and numbers, representing raw file data. As the cartographer points their quill, specific symbols transform into clear, readable text or simple icons, showing the editing process. Subtle digital particles and faint lines of code float in the air, connecting the parchment to a small, glowing screen displaying the hexhog logo. The scene is rendered in a detailed, digital painting style with a focus on the contrast between the old parchment and the subtle modern tech elements, emphasizing clarity and precision.

name: pluGET
description: Powerful Package manager that updates plugins & server software for Minecraft servers
readme_summary: pluGET is a Python-based package manager designed to automate the updating of plugins and server software for Minecraft servers. It supports local and remote server management via SFTP/FTP, integrating with APIs from Spigot, Modrinth, and GitHub to simplify plugin and server software maintenance.
key_features: Automated plugin and server software updates
Support for Spigot, Modrinth, and GitHub plugin sources
Updates for PaperMC, Purpur, Waterfall, and Velocity server software
Console-based operation with command line arguments
primary_use_case: Automating the process of updating plugins and server software for Minecraft servers to save time and ensure all components are up-to-date.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly robot librarian diligently organizing and updating shelves filled with glowing Minecraft plugin icons and server software boxes. The robot is using a tablet displaying a clean interface with version numbers and update indicators. Small digital particles representing data flow between the robot and the shelves. In the background, a subtle, stylized Minecraft server interface is visible. The scene is bright and clean, with a focus on efficiency and automation. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a vibrant color palette, emphasizing ease of use and the core function of automated updates.

name: librepods
readme_summary: LibrePods aims to liberate Apple AirPods features from the Apple ecosystem, enabling users to access premium functionalities like noise control, ear detection, and battery status on non-Apple devices, primarily Android and Linux.
primary_use_case: Enabling advanced AirPods features on non-Apple devices.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, open padlock with Apple AirPods nestled inside, emitting a vibrant, digital aura. From the padlock, streams of data flow outwards, transforming into icons representing various smart features like noise cancellation waves, a music note for ear detection, and a battery icon. The background is a clean, abstract digital landscape with subtle circuit board patterns. Small, floating UI elements resembling a smartphone settings menu are visible. The overall mood is one of liberation and enhanced functionality. The art style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a bright, modern color palette, emphasizing the unlocking of technology.
